format_note: Curated cooldown library, keyed by workout type slug. The app picks 'push' / 'pull' / 'legs' / etc. based on the active day's type. Per-day cooldown text authored in the Weekly Plan MD overrides this fallback.

apple_fitness_plus.note: On iPhone, this is a Universal Link — opens the Fitness app directly to the Mindful Cooldown category if installed. Filter inside the app for Upper Body / Lower Body / Full Body. 5–10 min sessions.

moves.cue: Forearm on door frame, elbow at shoulder height, step through until you feel chest stretch
Pull arm across body at chest height, use opposite hand at elbow
Side-lying, shoulder and elbow 90°, gently press forearm toward floor
Reach hand down between shoulder blades, use opposite hand to gently press elbow back
Walk hands forward, sink hips back, feel lat + shoulder opening
5–6 slow cycles, big breath each direction

pull.name: Pull-day cooldown (back / lats / rear delts / biceps)

moves.move: Lat hang (DB rack or pull-up bar)
moves.cue: Passive hang, let shoulders decompress
From child's pose, walk both hands to one side to stretch the opposite lat
Press chest up, shoulders down and back, gentle thoracic extension
Arm extended back at shoulder height, palm on wall, rotate body away
Quadruped, thread one arm under the body, rotate gently into the shoulder
5–6 slow cycles
Rear shin against bench/wall, front leg in lunge, sink hips and squeeze glute of stretching leg
Front shin perpendicular to body, sink hips, walk hands forward for deeper stretch
Foot on bench, hinge from hips with flat back, don't round
Front and back leg both at 90°, lean forward over front leg
Hands on wall, rear leg straight, heel down, press hips forward
Lying on back, ankle on opposite knee, pull thigh toward chest
Big toes touching, knees wide, sink hips toward heels

upper-hybrid.name: Upper-hybrid cooldown (shoulders / rear delts / arms / core)

moves.cue: Pull arm across body
Forearm on frame, step through
Passive hang, decompress shoulders
Hand between shoulder blades, opposite hand presses elbow
Quadruped, rotate one shoulder under
Gentle thoracic extension, shoulders down
Decompress spine, breathe deep
5–6 slow cycles
Thoracic extension
Hip opener
Soft knees, let head and arms hang
